sentences.quote: We present the first formal verification survey of the lattice chirality problem---the obstruction to placing chiral fermions on a lattice with the correct continuum limit. The formalization in Lean~4 with Mathlib comprises 968~theorems (zero axioms) across 66~modules (zero \texttt{sorry} in main modules), of which 273~are proved by the Aristotle automated theorem prover.
sentences.quote_normalized: we present the first formal verification survey of the lattice chirality problem---the obstruction to placing chiral fermions on a lattice with the correct continuum limit. the formalization in lean~4 with mathlib comprises 968~theorems (zero axioms) across 66~modules (zero sorry in main modules), of which 273~are proved by the aristotle automated theorem prover.
sentences.agent_notes: IA: abstract claims '968 theorems (zero axioms) across 66 modules ... 273 Aristotle' — current grep yields 2578 theorems, 1 axiom (gapped_interface_axiom), 170 modules, ARISTOTLE_PROVED_COUNT=322. Also internally inconsistent with paper's own Introduction (line 103: '2237+ theorems, 1 axiom, 130 modules, 322+ Aristotle'). FAIL.

sentences.quote: The work is organized around three complementary pillars.
sentences.quote_normalized: the work is organized around three complementary pillars.
sentences.agent_notes: Structural connector; no extractable claim.

sentences.quote: \textbf{Pillar~1 (No-Go):} The nine conditions of the Golterman-Shamir theorem are formalized as substantive propositions; five are shown violated by the Thorngren-Preskill-Fidkowski construction, establishing that the two frameworks operate in different mathematical regimes.
sentences.quote_normalized: pillar~1 (no-go): the nine conditions of the golterman-shamir theorem are formalized as substantive propositions; five are shown violated by the thorngren-preskill-fidkowski construction, establishing that the two frameworks operate in different mathematical regimes.
sentences.agent_notes: tpf_outside_gs_scope_main exists in lean_deps.json (TPFEvasion module). 'Five conditions violated' is the structural conjunction surfaced by potential_violation_count = 5 in TPFEvasion.lean (recorded WARN in v1 review, kept here for prose-walk).

sentences.quote: \textbf{Pillar~2 (Positive Construction):} The Gioia-Thorngren BdG Hamiltonian on a 3+1D lattice is formalized with Pauli matrix infrastructure, Wilson mass function, and Bogoliubov-de~Gennes Nambu structure. The central theorem $[H_{\mathrm{BdG}}(\mathbf{k}), q_A(\mathbf{k})] = 0$---exact chiral symmetry---is machine-verified at each momentum via a 2$\times$2 trigonometric identity ($\sin^2 + \cos^2 = 1$). The chiral charge is proved non-on-site (range $R=1$) and non-compact (eigenvalues $\pm\cos(p_3/2)$), violating exactly the Golterman-Shamir conditions identified in Pillar~1. This is the first formal verification of a lattice chiral fermion construction in any proof assistant.
sentences.quote_normalized: pillar~2 (positive construction): the gioia-thorngren bdg hamiltonian on a 3+1d lattice is formalized with pauli matrix infrastructure, wilson mass function, and bogoliubov-de~gennes nambu structure. the central theorem $[h_{bdg}(\mathbf{k}), q_a(\mathbf{k})] = 0$---exact chiral symmetry---is machine-verified at each momentum via a 2$\times$2 trigonometric identity ($\sin^2 + \cos^2 = 1$). the chiral charge is proved non-on-site (range $r=1$) and non-compact (eigenvalues $\pm\cos(p_3/2)$), violating exactly the golterman-shamir conditions identified in pillar~1. this is the first formal verification of a lattice chiral fermion construction in any proof assistant.
sentences.agent_notes: All three theorem refs exist in lean_deps.json. 'First formal verification' is a priority claim — outside structural verification scope; reproduces v1 WARN as candidate-for-supersession (LLM-judgment).

sentences.quote: \textbf{Pillar~3 (Algebraic Framework):} The Onsager algebra (first formalization in any proof assistant) is defined via the Dolan-Grady presentation, with the Davies isomorphism, Chevalley embedding into $L(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$, and In\"on\"u-Wigner contraction to $\mathfrak{su}(2)$ encoding the Witten anomaly on the lattice. The $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ classification of Pin$^+$ bordism is axiomatized, with the A(1) Steenrod subalgebra computation partially discharging the axiom.
sentences.quote_normalized: pillar~3 (algebraic framework): the onsager algebra (first formalization in any proof assistant) is defined via the dolan-grady presentation, with the davies isomorphism, chevalley embedding into $l(\mathfrak{sl}_2)$, and in\"on\"u-wigner contraction to $\mathfrak{su}(2)$ encoding the witten anomaly on the lattice. the $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ classification of pin$^+$ bordism is axiomatized, with the a(1) steenrod subalgebra computation partially discharging the axiom.

sentences.quote: Bridge theorems connect the Witten $\mathbb{Z}_2$ anomaly (element $8 \in \mathbb{Z}_{16}$) to the Onsager contraction and the Gioia-Thorngren Weyl doublet.

sentences.quote: A master synthesis theorem assembles all three pillars with explicit bridge connections, providing a complete machine-checked map of what is proved, what is axiomatized, and what remains open in the chirality wall problem.

sentences.quote: The question of whether chiral gauge theories---and in particular the Standard Model---can be consistently formulated on a lattice has resisted solution for over four decades. The Nielsen-Ninomiya theorem establishes that na\"ive lattice fermions with exact, on-site, quantized chiral symmetry necessarily produce equal numbers of left- and right-handed Weyl fermions.
sentences.quote_normalized: the question of whether chiral gauge theories---and in particular the standard model---can be consistently formulated on a lattice has resisted solution for over four decades. the nielsen-ninomiya theorem establishes that na\"ive lattice fermions with exact, on-site, quantized chiral symmetry necessarily produce equal numbers of left- and right-handed weyl fermions.
sentences.agent_notes: Citations Nielsen1981a / Nielsen1981b not present in CITATION_REGISTRY (citations.py). Bibliography entries exist but the registry-anchored chain is missing — Class IA-adjacent (citation-integrity).

sentences.quote: Circumventing this ``chirality wall'' is a prerequisite for non-perturbative lattice formulations of the Standard Model.
sentences.quote_normalized: circumventing this ``chirality wall'' is a prerequisite for non-perturbative lattice formulations of the standard model.
sentences.agent_notes: Standard motivational framing; not a structural claim.

sentences.quote: Three recent developments have transformed the landscape. First, Golterman and Shamir systematized the no-go into nine explicit conditions, under which any lattice Hamiltonian yields a vector-like spectrum. Second, Gioia and Thorngren constructed explicit 3+1D lattice Hamiltonians with exact chiral symmetry, evading Nielsen-Ninomiya by using non-on-site, non-compact charge operators. Third, Thorngren, Preskill, and Fidkowski showed that when 't~Hooft anomalies cancel, a constant-depth quantum circuit (symmetry disentangler) can make non-on-site symmetries on-site, enabling standard lattice gauging.
sentences.quote_normalized: three recent developments have transformed the landscape. first, golterman and shamir systematized the no-go into nine explicit conditions, under which any lattice hamiltonian yields a vector-like spectrum. second, gioia and thorngren constructed explicit 3+1d lattice hamiltonians with exact chiral symmetry, evading nielsen-ninomiya by using non-on-site, non-compact charge operators. third, thorngren, preskill, and fidkowski showed that when 't~hooft anomalies cancel, a constant-depth quantum circuit (symmetry disentangler) can make non-on-site symmetries on-site, enabling standard lattice gauging.
sentences.agent_notes: GoltermanShamir2026 in CITATION_REGISTRY ✓. GioiaTh2026 and TPF2026 NOT in CITATION_REGISTRY — citation-integrity gap.

sentences.quote: This paper presents a comprehensive formal verification of the chirality wall in the Lean~4 proof assistant with Mathlib. The formalization totals 2237+~theorems (1~axiom: \texttt{gapped\_interface\_axiom}) across 130~Lean modules, of which 322+ are proved by the Aristotle automated theorem prover. Phase~2 (3+1D extension) adds \texttt{GaugingStep.lean} (34~theorems: gauging obstruction, SMG phases, not-on-site symmetry formalization) and \texttt{SPTClassification.lean} (15~theorems + 1~axiom: SPT phase types, gapped interface conjecture, conditional chiral gauge theorem).
sentences.quote_normalized: this paper presents a comprehensive formal verification of the chirality wall in the lean~4 proof assistant with mathlib. the formalization totals 2237+~theorems (1~axiom: gapped\_interface\_axiom) across 130~lean modules, of which 322+ are proved by the aristotle automated theorem prover. phase~2 (3+1d extension) adds gaugingstep.lean (34~theorems: gauging obstruction, smg phases, not-on-site symmetry formalization) and sptclassification.lean (15~theorems + 1~axiom: spt phase types, gapped interface conjecture, conditional chiral gauge theorem).

sentences.quote: The work is organized around three pillars: the no-go (Sec.~\ref{sec:pillar1}), the positive construction (Sec.~\ref{sec:pillar2}), and the algebraic framework (Sec.~\ref{sec:pillar3}). Bridge theorems connecting the pillars are presented in Sec.~\ref{sec:bridges}, and a master synthesis theorem is given in Sec.~\ref{sec:master}.

sentences.quote: Gioia and Thorngren construct a single Weyl fermion via a modified Karsten-Wilczek Hamiltonian in the Bogoliubov-de~Gennes (Nambu) formalism. At each momentum $\mathbf{k}$ on the discrete Brillouin zone $(\mathrm{Fin}\;L)^3$, the BdG Hamiltonian is a $4\times4$ matrix in the tensor product $\sigma$ (spin) $\otimes$ $\tau$ (Nambu): [eq:bdg] [eq:heff] with Wilson mass $W(\mathbf{p}) = 2 - \cos p_1 - \cos p_2$ and Wilson parameter $r$. This is formalized in \texttt{BdGHamiltonian.lean} (8~theorems).
sentences.quote_normalized: gioia and thorngren construct a single weyl fermion via a modified karsten-wilczek hamiltonian in the bogoliubov-de~gennes (nambu) formalism. at each momentum $\mathbf{k}$ on the discrete brillouin zone $(fin\;l)^3$, the bdg hamiltonian is a $4\times4$ matrix in the tensor product $\sigma$ (spin) $\otimes$ $\tau$ (nambu): [eq:bdg] [eq:heff] with wilson mass $w(\mathbf{p}) = 2 - \cos p_1 - \cos p_2$ and wilson parameter $r$. this is formalized in bdghamiltonian.lean (8~theorems).
sentences.agent_notes: BdGHamiltonian.lean theorem count = 8 (matches). GioiaTh2026 NOT in CITATION_REGISTRY — citation-integrity caveat. WARN due to citation gap; structural count agrees.

sentences.quote: The full Wilson mass $M(\mathbf{k}) = 3 - \cos k_x - \cos k_y - \cos k_z$ gaps all doubler fermions. The key property, proved in \texttt{WilsonMass.lean} (11~theorems): [eq:wilson_zero] for all finite lattice sizes $L \geq 1$. The proof uses $\cos\theta \leq 1$ for all $\theta$ (\texttt{Real.cos\_le\_one}): the equation $\cos k_x + \cos k_y + \cos k_z = 3$ forces each cosine to equal~1. This ensures exactly one massless Weyl node---a chiral, not vector-like, spectrum.
sentences.quote_normalized: the full wilson mass $m(\mathbf{k}) = 3 - \cos k_x - \cos k_y - \cos k_z$ gaps all doubler fermions. the key property, proved in wilsonmass.lean (11~theorems): [eq:wilson_zero] for all finite lattice sizes $l \geq 1$. the proof uses $\cos\theta \leq 1$ for all $\theta$ (real.cos\_le\_one): the equation $\cos k_x + \cos k_y + \cos k_z = 3$ forces each cosine to equal~1. this ensures exactly one massless weyl node---a chiral, not vector-like, spectrum.

sentences.quote: The chiral charge at momentum $\mathbf{p}$ acts as identity in spin space: [eq:chiral_charge] This operator is non-on-site (depends on $p_3$, real-space range $R=1$, nearest-neighbor along $\hat{z}$); non-compact (eigenvalues $\pm\cos(p_3/2)$, a continuous non-integer spectrum); Ginsparg-Wilson ($q_A^2 = \cos^2(p_3/2)\;\mathbf{1}_4$).

sentences.quote: The proof decomposes into three parts: the $\sigma_1\!\otimes\!\mathbf{1}$ and $\sigma_3\!\otimes\!\mathbf{1}$ terms commute with $\mathbf{1}\!\otimes\! q_\tau$ by the mixed Kronecker identity $[A\!\otimes\!\mathbf{1},\,\mathbf{1}\!\otimes\! B]=0$. The scalar part $r\,W\,\mathbf{1}_\tau$ of $h_{\mathrm{eff}}$ commutes trivially. The $\tau$-dependent part reduces to a $2\times2$ identity [eq:tau_identity].
sentences.quote_normalized: the proof decomposes into three parts: the $\sigma_1\!\otimes\!\mathbf{1}$ and $\sigma_3\!\otimes\!\mathbf{1}$ terms commute with $\mathbf{1}\!\otimes\! q_\tau$ by the mixed kronecker identity $[a\!\otimes\!\mathbf{1},\,\mathbf{1}\!\otimes\! b]=0$. the scalar part $r\,w\,\mathbf{1}_\tau$ of $h_{eff}$ commutes trivially. the $\tau$-dependent part reduces to a $2\times2$ identity [eq:tau_identity].
sentences.agent_notes: Three-part decomposition matches Lean proof structure; gt_tau_commutator_vanishes is the 2×2 reduction.

sentences.quote: Equation~(\ref{eq:tau_identity}) expands via $[\tau_z,\tau_x]=2i\tau_y$ to $i\sin^2\!p_3\,\tau_y - i(1\!-\!\cos^2\!p_3)\,\tau_y = 0$, which is the Pythagorean identity in disguise.
sentences.quote_normalized: equation~() expands via $[\tau_z,\tau_x]=2i\tau_y$ to $i\sin^2\!p_3\,\tau_y - i(1\!-\!\cos^2\!p_3)\,\tau_y = 0$, which is the pythagorean identity in disguise.

sentences.quote: The Pauli matrices $\sigma_x$, $\sigma_y$, $\sigma_z$ are defined as explicit $2\times2$ complex matrices in \texttt{PauliMatrices.lean} (15~theorems). The commutation relations $[\sigma_i,\sigma_j] = 2i\,\varepsilon_{ijk}\,\sigma_k$, anti-commutation $\{\sigma_i,\sigma_j\}=2\delta_{ij}$, involutivity $\sigma_i^2=\mathbf{1}$, and tracelessness are all machine-verified. This infrastructure is reusable for any future lattice fermion formalization.

sentences.quote: In \texttt{OnsagerContraction.lean} (12~theorems), the In\"on\"u-Wigner contraction $\mathcal{O} \to \mathfrak{su}(2)$ is formalized. sentences.quote: The A(1) Steenrod subalgebra---the first formalization of any Steenrod algebra in a proof assistant---partially discharges the axiom via the finite computation $\dim_{\mathbb{F}_2} {\rm Ext}^4_{A(1)}(\mathbb{F}_2,\mathbb{F}_2) = 3$ (\texttt{A1Ring.lean}+\texttt{A1Resolution.lean}+\texttt{A1Ext.lean}, first machine-checked Ext computation over any Steenrod subalgebra). The number 16 enters through the infinite $h_0$-tower in stem~4 assembling to~$\mathbb{Z}$ via Adams spectral sequence extensions.
sentences.quote_normalized: the a(1) steenrod subalgebra---the first formalization of any steenrod algebra in a proof assistant---partially discharges the axiom via the finite computation $\dim_{\mathbb{f}_2} {\rm ext}^4_{a(1)}(\mathbb{f}_2,\mathbb{f}_2) = 3$ (a1ring.lean+a1resolution.lean+a1ext.lean, first machine-checked ext computation over any steenrod subalgebra). the number 16 enters through the infinite $h_0$-tower in stem~4 assembling to~$\mathbb{z}$ via adams spectral sequence extensions.
sentences.agent_notes: Files A1Ring.lean / A1Resolution.lean / A1Ext.lean exist (verified by ls). Priority claim 'first formalization of any Steenrod algebra in a proof assistant' is interpretive — reproduces v1 WARN as candidate-for-supersession.

sentences.quote: The Witten SU(2) anomaly ($\pi_4(SU(2)) = \mathbb{Z}_2$) embeds as element $8 \in \mathbb{Z}_{16}$---the unique element of order~2. In the GT Weyl doublet (Model~2), the two charges $Q_V$ (on-site) and $Q_A$ (non-on-site) generate the Onsager algebra on the lattice, which contracts to $SU(2)$ in the IR. This emanant $SU(2)$ carries the Witten anomaly, protecting gaplessness. This chain is formalized in \texttt{GTWeylDoublet.lean} (12~theorems) [eq:anomaly_chain] with anomaly cancellation requiring $16n$ Majorana fermions.
sentences.quote_normalized: the witten su(2) anomaly ($\pi_4(su(2)) = \mathbb{z}_2$) embeds as element $8 \in \mathbb{z}_{16}$---the unique element of order~2. in the gt weyl doublet (model~2), the two charges $q_v$ (on-site) and $q_a$ (non-on-site) generate the onsager algebra on the lattice, which contracts to $su(2)$ in the ir. this emanant $su(2)$ carries the witten anomaly, protecting gaplessness. this chain is formalized in gtweyldoublet.lean (12~theorems) [eq:anomaly_chain] with anomaly cancellation requiring $16n$ majorana fermions.
sentences.agent_notes: GTWeylDoublet.lean theorem count = 12 (matches). 8 ∈ Z_16 as unique order-2 element is structural arithmetic ✓. Witten1982 NOT in CITATION_REGISTRY — citation gap.

sentences.quote: Pillar~1$\to$2 (GS$\to$GT): The GT construction violates exactly the conditions the GS no-go requires---non-on-site $Q_A$ (condition C6/I2) and non-compact spectrum (condition I3). The evasion is precise.
sentences.quote_normalized: pillar~1$\to$2 (gs$\to$gt): the gt construction violates exactly the conditions the gs no-go requires---non-on-site $q_a$ (condition c6/i2) and non-compact spectrum (condition i3). the evasion is precise.
sentences.agent_notes: Both supporting theorems exist; bridge is consistent with Pillar 2 sec.

sentences.quote: Pillar~2$\to$3 (GT$\to$Anomaly): The GT doublet generates the Onsager algebra ($\mathrm{DG\_COEFF}=16$), which contracts to $\mathfrak{su}(2)$ ($\dim=3$), carrying the Witten $\mathbb{Z}_2$ (element $8 \in \mathbb{Z}_{16}$). Anomaly cancellation at $16n$ Majorana connects to the TPF gauging pipeline.

sentences.quote: Pillar~1$\to$3 (GS$\to\mathbb{Z}_{16}$): Together, the GS no-go and $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ constrain the chirality wall from both sides: any lattice construction must either violate GS conditions or use $16n$ fermions for anomaly cancellation.

sentences.quote: Two former axioms are now proved as theorems: the GS no-go theorem and gauge erasure universality (non-abelian center discrete). A third former axiom---$\Omega_5^{\mathrm{Spin}^{\mathbb{Z}_4}} \cong \mathbb{Z}_{16}$---was converted to a trivially-true placeholder theorem (\texttt{dai\_freed\_spin\_z4}) pending the addition of cobordism theory to Mathlib; the cobordism computation itself is an external input, as is standard in the $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ literature.
sentences.quote_normalized: two former axioms are now proved as theorems: the gs no-go theorem and gauge erasure universality (non-abelian center discrete). a third former axiom---$\omega_5^{spin^{\mathbb{z}_4}} \cong \mathbb{z}_{16}$---was converted to a trivially-true placeholder theorem (dai\_freed\_spin\_z4) pending the addition of cobordism theory to mathlib; the cobordism computation itself is an external input, as is standard in the $\mathbb{z}_{16}$ literature.

sentences.quote: \noindent\textbf{What is proved:} 2237+ theorems across 130 Lean modules, including $[H,Q_A]=0$, Wilson mass uniqueness, Pauli algebra, Onsager relations, contraction, $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ conditional theorems, A(1) Steenrod Ext computation (first in any proof assistant), GS conditions, TPF evasion, SM anomaly in $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$, generation constraint, Drinfeld center computations, Fidkowski-Kitaev 2+1D gapped interface (first FK formalization), and all bridge connections.

sentences.quote: \noindent\textbf{Machine-checked evidence for the gapped interface:} The analogous statement is machine-verified in two lower dimensions: (i) in 1+1D via the 3450 K-matrix model (\texttt{VillainHamiltonian.lean}), and (ii) in 2+1D via the Fidkowski-Kitaev construction (\texttt{FKGappedInterface.lean}, 20~theorems)---the first machine-checked proof that 8~Majorana fermions can be gapped to a unique ground state (spectral gap $\Delta=2$) while preserving symmetry.
sentences.quote_normalized: \noindentmachine-checked evidence for the gapped interface: the analogous statement is machine-verified in two lower dimensions: (i) in 1+1d via the 3450 k-matrix model (villainhamiltonian.lean), and (ii) in 2+1d via the fidkowski-kitaev construction (fkgappedinterface.lean, 20~theorems)---the first machine-checked proof that 8~majorana fermions can be gapped to a unique ground state (spectral gap $\delta=2$) while preserving symmetry.
sentences.agent_notes: FKGappedInterface.lean theorem count = 20 (matches). VillainHamiltonian.lean exists. FK2010 / FK2011 NOT in CITATION_REGISTRY — citation gap. 'first machine-checked' is priority claim.

sentences.quote: \noindent\textbf{What remains open:} The 4+1D gapped interface conjecture (TPF, the project's single axiom), the gauging step from vector-like SMG to chiral gauge coupling, and the full $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$ cobordism proof (algebraic core machine-checked via the Ext computation; 3~topological hypotheses remain).

sentences.quote: The formalization demonstrates that the chirality wall is ``fracturing under coordinated assault'': the no-go's scope is limited (5/9 conditions violable), an explicit construction evades it (GT, machine-verified), and the anomaly framework explains why the evasion works (Onsager $\to$ $\mathfrak{su}(2)$ $\to$ Witten $\to$ $\mathbb{Z}_{16}$). Three critical gaps remain, but the formal framework on which any resolution must stand is now machine-checked.
sentences.quote_normalized: the formalization demonstrates that the chirality wall is ``fracturing under coordinated assault'': the no-go's scope is limited (5/9 conditions violable), an explicit construction evades it (gt, machine-verified), and the anomaly framework explains why the evasion works (onsager $\to$ $\mathfrak{su}(2)$ $\to$ witten $\to$ $\mathbb{z}_{16}$). three critical gaps remain, but the formal framework on which any resolution must stand is now machine-checked.

sentences.quote: We have presented the first comprehensive formal verification of the lattice chirality problem in Lean~4, comprising 2237+~theorems (1~axiom: gapped interface) across 130~modules. The three-pillar structure---no-go, positive construction, anomaly classification---provides a complete machine-checked map of the chirality wall as of 2026. The Gioia-Thorngren chiral symmetry $[H,Q_A]=0$ is formally verified, the Onsager algebra is formalized for the first time, and all bridge connections between the three pillars are machine-checked.
